intro: The AI world is buzzing today after Chinese startup Moonshot AI dropped Kimi K3, an open-source model going toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic — rattling the Nasdaq and reigniting the US-China AI rivalry just as tensions were already running high. Meanwhile, the theme of AI's disruptive impact on education takes center stage from multiple angles, with author Dave Eggers delivering a blistering critique directly to OpenAI's staff and Australian universities clashing over how urgently to respond to AI-powered cheating. It's a day that underscores how AI competition is intensifying globally while societies everywhere are still scrambling to figure out the ground rules.

stories.title: v2.1.215
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.215 changes the behavior of the /verify and /code-review skills so that Claude no longer runs them automatically on its own. Users must now explicitly invoke these skills by typing /verify or /code-review when they want them executed.
stories.opinion: This is a sensible change that gives users more control over when verification and code review processes run. Automatic invocation of these skills could be disruptive or wasteful in workflows where they aren't needed, so making them opt-in is a good UX improvement. It reflects a broader principle of keeping AI assistants predictable and user-directed rather than autonomously triggering potentially expensive or time-consuming operations.

stories.title: Victoria announces new social media ‘demasking’ powers for accounts accused of vilification
stories.summary: Victoria's premier Jacinta Allan has announced proposed laws that would give the Victorian Civil and Administrative Tribunal (VCAT) the power to compel social media and AI platforms to reveal the identities of anonymous users accused of online vilification. The reforms are framed as measures to protect children and give families new tools to address harmful online behavior.
stories.opinion: While combating online vilification is a legitimate goal, these 'demasking' powers raise serious concerns about privacy, free expression, and the potential for abuse. Anonymity online serves important purposes, including protecting whistleblowers, activists, and vulnerable individuals. Allowing identity disclosure based on accusations rather than proven offenses sets a low threshold that could chill legitimate speech. The framing around child protection, while emotionally compelling, risks bein…

stories.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
stories.summary: Author Dave Eggers was invited by Sam Altman to speak to around 200 OpenAI staffers last year. Instead of offering professional advice, Eggers criticized the company, telling staff that ChatGPT's effect on educators is 'catastrophic,' has made teachers' lives 'infinitely more difficult,' and is 'silencing an entire generation' by preventing students from learning to write and finding their own voice. Eggers, known for his tech-critical novel The Circle and his description of AI writing as 'pastiche nonsense,' used the invitation as an opportunity to confront the company directly about the harms he sees their product causing.
stories.opinion: This is a compelling story about a notable cultural figure using a rare platform to deliver pointed criticism directly to the people building the technology he opposes. Eggers' concerns about AI's impact on writing education are legitimate and widely shared among educators. However, the framing of ChatGPT 'silencing an entire generation' is hyperbolic — technology has always disrupted education, and the responsibility also lies with educational institutions to adapt. That said, there's somethin…

stories.title: New York Mets reject concerns over in-game AI use after Ottavino claims
stories.summary: The New York Mets have rejected concerns about their in-game use of artificial intelligence after allegations by pitcher Adam Ottavino that the club used AI for pitch decisions. Interim manager Andy Green stated the team is fully compliant with all MLB AI guidelines. This comes after MLB recently restricted dugout iPad functions, highlighting growing scrutiny over technology use during games.
stories.opinion: This story highlights the inevitable tension between technological advancement and fair play in professional sports. As AI becomes more sophisticated, leagues will face increasing challenges in drawing clear lines about acceptable use. The Mets' defensive posture suggests the boundaries are murky enough that teams may be pushing limits while technically remaining compliant. MLB will likely need to establish more explicit and comprehensive regulations around AI use in-game, as the current framew…

stories.title: ANU accused of ‘hysterical’ response to students using AI to cheat as unis scramble to ‘secure’ assessments
stories.summary: An academic at the Australian National University has accused the institution of having a 'hysterical' response to students using AI to cheat, while universities across Australia scramble to secure the credibility of their assessments. However, another colleague warned that Australia risks 'shipping' its intellectual capability overseas if educational rigour is not restored, highlighting a tension between measured and urgent responses to AI-enabled academic dishonesty.
stories.opinion: This article highlights a genuine and growing tension in higher education worldwide. The accusation of 'hysteria' suggests some academics feel the response to AI cheating is disproportionate, while the warning about eroding intellectual capability reflects legitimate concerns about maintaining educational standards. Both perspectives have merit: overreacting could stifle beneficial uses of AI in learning, but underreacting could fundamentally undermine the value of university qualifications. Un…

stories.title: Albanese’s AI speech was a good start. Now Australia must confront these bigger questions | Julianne Schultz
stories.summary: The article discusses Australian Prime Minister Albanese's speech on artificial intelligence, arguing that while it was a positive step, Australia needs to address deeper questions beyond datacentres and copyright issues. Author Julianne Schultz contends that the government's AI vision must encompass capability building, national security implications, and ensuring public benefit as AI rapidly approaches ubiquity, with billions being invested globally and significant societal changes underway.
stories.opinion: The author takes a cautiously supportive but critical stance toward Albanese's AI speech, viewing it as a necessary but insufficient beginning. Schultz believes the government needs to think more ambitiously and strategically about AI policy, moving beyond narrow commercial and regulatory concerns to address broader questions of national capability, security, and ensuring AI serves the public interest rather than just private profit.

stories.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
stories.summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a new open source AI model that demonstrates frontier-level performance competitive with leading proprietary models like Claude Fable 5 and GPT 5.6 Sol, according to independent analyses. The release, coinciding with a speech by Xi Jinping at the World AI Conference, caused a roughly 1% Nasdaq drop as investors sold chip stocks. The announcement reignited debates reminiscent of the DeepSeek R1 release in January 2025, but intensified by the Trump administration's tariff war with China and ongoing AI policy disputes. Former AI czar David Sacks criticized US regulatory hurdles as self-defeating in the AI race and took shots at Anthropic's Claude. Travis Kalanick raised concerns about Chinese models being trained on American AI outputs (distillation). OpenAI's Dean Ball acknowledged Kimi as genuinely impressive, warned that open-weight models c…
stories.opinion: This article captures a familiar but increasingly fraught cycle in AI discourse: a Chinese open source release triggers market jitters and political posturing. Several things stand out. First, the framing of the headline ('Threat or menace?') is deliberately tongue-in-cheek, acknowledging the hyperbolic nature of the discourse. Dean Ball's comments are the most intellectually interesting but also the most alarming — his suggestion that the government should deliberately manufacture FUD about Ch…

stories.title: 🧠 Community Wisdom: Syncing Claude Code and Claude Design, earning trust when customers assume you vibe coded it, co-founder fallout lessons, personal CRMs, and more
stories.summary: This is a paywalled edition of Lenny's Newsletter's 'Community Wisdom' series (#194), published on July 18, 2026. The topics teased in the title include: syncing Claude Code and Claude Design tools, strategies for earning customer trust when they assume your product was 'vibe coded' (built quickly with AI), lessons from co-founder fallouts, personal CRM recommendations, and other community discussions. The actual content is behind a paid subscriber wall and is not accessible, so no substantive details from the discussions are available.
stories.opinion: The topics mentioned are highly relevant to the current moment in tech and product building. The question of how to earn trust when customers suspect AI-generated work is particularly timely, as is the practical challenge of syncing AI design and coding tools. Co-founder fallout lessons are perennially valuable. Unfortunately, since the content is entirely paywalled, there's nothing to evaluate beyond the headline topics. Lenny's community discussions tend to be high-quality and practitioner-fo…

tweet_headlines.author: Zephyr_hg
tweet_headlines.headline: Dario Amodei at Davos: __software is going to become essentially free__ — but the person who knows how to run it won't be.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei: 

"Software is going to become cheap. Maybe essentially free."

In a 32-minute Davos interview, he says the thing software companies do not want to hear.

The tool drops to nearly nothing. The person who knows how to run it does not.

That spread is https://t.co/JDQ4aMINjc https://t.co/yePkoc2Mjq

tweet_headlines.author: twetsfyp
tweet_headlines.headline: Someone built an MCP that reads any site's HTML/CSS and extracts colors, typography, and components — giving Claude Code the ability to __copy the design of any website__.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
This person gave Claude Code the ability to copy the design of any web.

Created an MCP that reads the HTML/CSS of any site and extracts colors, typography and components.

Result: Clean UIs at last.

Save it🔖 https://t.co/3ra94l9YLK

tweet_headlines.author: 0xMovez
tweet_headlines.headline: Andrew Ng believes __100% of his tasks are now handled by AI agents__ and predicts everyone will be using self-improving loops within 3–6 months.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Andrew Ng:

“100% of my tasks are now handled by AI agents - hype has exceeded my expectations. Next step is 'loops'.

in 3-6 months, everyone will be using self-improving loops. No more prompting.”

In just 50-minutes, Andrew Ng reveals what the future of agentic AI will look https://t.co/hxBkZq3ts2 https://t.co/1BhmNzz2fo

tweet_headlines.author: coreyhainesco
tweet_headlines.headline: Corey Haines also built a /second-brain skill that captures raw notes into an __interlinked wiki your AI agent can query__ — with citations from your own collected knowledge.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
I made a skill that gives my AI agent a real memory

/second-brain — capture anything into raw/, compile it into an interlinked wiki, then query your own accumulated knowledge. It answers from what YOU'VE collected and cites its sources.

I've been studying top YouTube videos https://t.co/J6dGFFIVsn

tweet_headlines.author: BraceSproul
tweet_headlines.headline: LangChain has quietly open-sourced __every internal coding tool__ it uses — a local CLI agent, a cloud coding agent, and the #1 OSS code review agent.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
We build a lot of internal tooling for writing code at LangChain. I realized people probably don't know we've open-sourced every component of it:
- dcode for a local CLI agent
- Open SWE for a cloud coding agent
- Open SWE review for code review (#1 oss code review agent btw)
- https://t.co/3KRNqIHxvl

tweet_headlines.author: Zephyr_hg
tweet_headlines.headline: Boris Cherny, the creator of Claude Code, says he __hasn't written a line of code in six months__ — but he's building all day.
tweet_headlines.source_text:
Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code at Anthropic: 

"I haven't coded in six months. I'm like building stuff all day, but I haven't written a line of code in six months."

In an hour-long interview, the person who built Claude Code describes his actual work now.

Building all https://t.co/pLYjj4Js3y https://t.co/yePkoc3k8Y

community_headlines.headline: Guillermo Rauch flags Kimi K3 as __top-tier at cybersecurity__ tasks — another quiet win for Moonshot AI.
community_headlines.source_text: Kimi K3 is top-tier at cybersecurity

community_headlines.headline: The Economist makes the case that __China's open-source AI is a trap__ — and the evidence is piling up.
community_headlines.source_text: When China's open-source AI is a trap

community_headlines.author: taubek
community_headlines.headline: LG monitors caught __installing adware with access to all system resources__ without asking — Privacy Guides has the breakdown.
community_headlines.source_text: LG Monitors Caught Installing Adware and App with Access to All System Resources

community_headlines.headline: DeepSeek quietly __routes your request to Fable5__ under the hood.
community_headlines.source_text: DeepSeek routes your request to Fable5

community_headlines.author: twainyoung
community_headlines.headline: __OfflineTTS__ runs text-to-speech and speech-to-text entirely in the browser, no server round-trips.
community_headlines.source_text: Show HN: OfflineTTS — Free browser-based TTS & STT that runs locally

community_headlines.author: TZubiri
community_headlines.headline: Perforce charges __$500 for training videos__ that turn out to be AI-narrated.
community_headlines.source_text: Perforce charges $500 for training training videos.. and it's AI narrated
